verses.latin_vulgate: Videns autem populus quod moram faceret descendendi de monte Moyses, congregatus adversus Aaron, dixit : Surge, fac nobis deos, qui nos præcedant : Moysi enim huic viro, qui nos eduxit de terra Ægypti, ignoramus quid acciderit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֣רְא הָעָ֔ם כִּֽי־בֹשֵׁ֥שׁ מֹשֶׁ֖ה לָרֶ֣דֶת מִן־הָהָ֑ר וַיִּקָּהֵ֨ל הָעָ֜ם עַֽל־אַהֲרֹ֗ן וַיֹּאמְר֤וּ אֵלָיו֙ ק֣וּם ׀ עֲשֵׂה־לָ֣נוּ אֱלֹהִ֗ים אֲשֶׁ֤ר יֵֽלְכוּ֙ לְפָנֵ֔ינוּ כִּי־זֶ֣ה ׀ מֹשֶׁ֣ה הָאִ֗ישׁ אֲשֶׁ֤ר הֶֽעֱלָ֙נוּ֙ מֵאֶ֣רֶץ מִצְרַ֔יִם לֹ֥א יָדַ֖עְנוּ מֶה־הָ֥יָה לֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the people seeing that Moses delayed to come down from the mount, gathering together against Aaron, said: Arise, make us gods, that may go before us: For as to this Moses, the man that brought us out of the land of Egypt, we know not what has befallen him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta koska kansa näki, että Moses viipyi tulemasta alas vuorelta, niin he kokoontuivat Aaronia vastaan, ja sanoivat hänelle: nouse, tee meille jumalia, jotka meidän edellämme kävisivät; sillä emme tiedä, mitä tälle miehelle Mosekselle tapahtunut on, joka meidät Egyptin maalta johdatti.
verses.king_james: And when the people saw that Moses delayed to come down out of the mount, the people gathered themselves together unto Aaron, and said unto him, Up, make us gods, which shall go before us; for as for this Moses, the man that brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ad eos Aaron : Tollite inaures aureas de uxorum, filiorumque et filiarum vestrarum auribus, et afferte ad me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר אֲלֵהֶם֙ אַהֲרֹ֔ן פָּֽרְקוּ֙ נִזְמֵ֣י הַזָּהָ֔ב אֲשֶׁר֙ בְּאׇזְנֵ֣י נְשֵׁיכֶ֔ם בְּנֵיכֶ֖ם וּבְנֹתֵיכֶ֑ם וְהָבִ֖יאוּ אֵלָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Aaron said to them: Take the golden earrings from the ears of your wives, and your sons and daughters, and bring them to me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi Aaron heille: reväiskäät kultaiset korvarenkaat, jotka ovat emäntienne korvissa, ja teidän poikainne, ja teidän tyttärenne: ja tuokaat minun tyköni.
verses.king_james: And Aaron said unto them, Break off the golden earrings, which are in the ears of your wives, of your sons, and of your daughters, and bring them unto me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fecitque populus quæ jusserat, deferens inaures ad Aaron.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתְפָּֽרְקוּ֙ כׇּל־הָעָ֔ם אֶת־נִזְמֵ֥י הַזָּהָ֖ב אֲשֶׁ֣ר בְּאׇזְנֵיהֶ֑ם וַיָּבִ֖יאוּ אֶֽל־אַהֲרֹֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the people did what he had commanded, bringing the earrings to Aaron.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin kaikki kansa repäisi ne kultaiset korvarenkaat, jotka olivat heidän korvissansa: ja toivat Aaronin tykö.
verses.king_james: And all the people brake off the golden earrings which were in their ears, and brought them unto Aaron.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quas cum ille accepisset, formavit opere fusorio, et fecit ex eis vitulum conflatilem : dixeruntque : Hi sunt dii tui Israël, qui te eduxerunt de terra Ægypti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקַּ֣ח מִיָּדָ֗ם וַיָּ֤צַר אֹתוֹ֙ בַּחֶ֔רֶט וַֽיַּעֲשֵׂ֖הוּ עֵ֣גֶל מַסֵּכָ֑ה וַיֹּ֣אמְר֔וּ אֵ֤לֶּה אֱלֹהֶ֙יךָ֙ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אֲשֶׁ֥ר הֶעֱל֖וּךָ מֵאֶ֥רֶץ מִצְרָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when he had received them, he fashioned them by founders' work, and made of them a molten calf. And they said: These are thy gods, O Israel, that have brought thee out of the land of Egypt.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jonka hän otti heidän kädestänsä, ja kuvasi sen kaivinraudalla, ja teki siitä valetun vasikan; ja he sanoivat: nämät ovat sinun jumalas, Israel, jotka sinun Egyptin maalta johdattivat ulos.
verses.king_james: And he received them at their hand, and fashioned it with a graving tool, after he had made it a molten calf: and they said, These be thy gods, O Israel, which brought thee up out of the land of Egypt.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod cum vidisset Aaron, ædificavit altare coram eo, et præconis voce clamavit dicens : Cras solemnitas Domini est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֣רְא אַהֲרֹ֔ן וַיִּ֥בֶן מִזְבֵּ֖חַ לְפָנָ֑יו וַיִּקְרָ֤א אַֽהֲרֹן֙ וַיֹּאמַ֔ר חַ֥ג לַיהֹוָ֖ה מָחָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when Aaron saw this, he built an altar before it, and made proclamation by a crier's voice, saying To morrow is the solemnity of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja koska Aaron sen näki, rakensi hän alttarin heidän eteensä; ja Aaron huusi ja sanoi: huomenna on Herran juhlapäivä.
verses.king_james: And when Aaron saw it, he built an altar before it; and Aaron made proclamation, and said, To morrow is a feast to the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surgentesque mane, obtulerunt holocausta, et hostias pacificas, et sedit populus manducare, et bibere, et surrexerunt ludere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּשְׁכִּ֙ימוּ֙ מִֽמׇּחֳרָ֔ת וַיַּעֲל֣וּ עֹלֹ֔ת וַיַּגִּ֖שׁוּ שְׁלָמִ֑ים וַיֵּ֤שֶׁב הָעָם֙ לֶֽאֱכֹ֣ל וְשָׁת֔וֹ וַיָּקֻ֖מוּ לְצַחֵֽק׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And rising in the morning, they offered holocausts, and peace victims, and the people sat down to eat and drink, and they rose up to play.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he varhain aamulla nousivat toisena päivänä, ja he uhrasivat polttouhria, ja kantoivat edes kiitosuhria. Ja kansa istui syömään ja juomaan, ja nousivat mässäämään.
verses.king_james: And they rose up early on the morrow, and offered burnt offerings, and brought peace offerings; and the people sat down to eat and to drink, and rose up to play.

verses.latin_vulgate: Locutus est autem Dominus ad Moysen, dicens : Vade, descende : peccavit populus tuus, quem eduxisti de terra Ægypti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְדַבֵּ֥ר יְהֹוָ֖ה אֶל־מֹשֶׁ֑ה לֶךְ־רֵ֕ד כִּ֚י שִׁחֵ֣ת עַמְּךָ֔ אֲשֶׁ֥ר הֶעֱלֵ֖יתָ מֵאֶ֥רֶץ מִצְרָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying: Go, get thee down: thy people, which thou hast brought out of the land of Egypt, hath sinned.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Herra puhui Mosekselle: mene, astu alas; sillä sinun kansas, jonka sinä Egyptin maalta johdatit ulos, turmeli itsensä.
verses.king_james: And the Lord said unto Moses, Go, get thee down; for thy people, which thou broughtest out of the land of Egypt, have corrupted themselves:

verses.latin_vulgate: Recesserunt cito de via, quam ostendisti eis : feceruntque sibi vitulum conflatilem, et adoraverunt, atque immolantes ei hostias, dixerunt : Isti sunt dii tui Israël, qui te eduxerunt de terra Ægypti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: סָ֣רוּ מַהֵ֗ר מִן־הַדֶּ֙רֶךְ֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר צִוִּיתִ֔ם עָשׂ֣וּ לָהֶ֔ם עֵ֖גֶל מַסֵּכָ֑ה וַיִּשְׁתַּֽחֲווּ־לוֹ֙ וַיִּזְבְּחוּ־ל֔וֹ וַיֹּ֣אמְר֔וּ אֵ֤לֶּה אֱלֹהֶ֙יךָ֙ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אֲשֶׁ֥ר הֶֽעֱל֖וּךָ מֵאֶ֥רֶץ מִצְרָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: They have quickly strayed from the way which thou didst shew them: and they have made to themselves a molten calf, and have adored it, and sacrificing victims to it, have said: These are thy gods, O Israel, that have brought thee out of the land of Egypt.
verses.finnish_biblia: He äkisti poikkesivat pois siltä tieltä, jonka minä heille käskin. He tekivät itsellensä valetun vasikan, ja kumarsivat sitä, ja uhrasivat sille, ja sanoivat: nämät ovat sinun jumalas, Israel, jotka sinun Egyptin maalta johdattivat ulos.
verses.king_james: They have turned aside quickly out of the way which I commanded them: they have made them a molten calf, and have worshipped it, and have sacrificed thereunto, and said, These be thy gods, O Israel, which have brought thee up out of the land of Egypt.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rursumque ait Dominus ad Moysen : Cerno quod populus iste duræ cervicis sit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר יְהֹוָ֖ה אֶל־מֹשֶׁ֑ה רָאִ֙יתִי֙ אֶת־הָעָ֣ם הַזֶּ֔ה וְהִנֵּ֥ה עַם־קְשֵׁה־עֹ֖רֶף הֽוּא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And again the Lord said to Moses: I see that this people is stiffnecked:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra sanoi Mosekselle: minä näin tämän kansan; ja katso, se on niskurikansa.
verses.king_james: And the Lord said unto Moses, I have seen this people, and, behold, it is a stiffnecked people:

verses.latin_vulgate: dimitte me, ut irascatur furor meus contra eos, et deleam eos, faciamque te in gentem magnam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּה֙ הַנִּ֣יחָה לִּ֔י וְיִֽחַר־אַפִּ֥י בָהֶ֖ם וַאֲכַלֵּ֑ם וְאֶֽעֱשֶׂ֥ה אוֹתְךָ֖ לְג֥וֹי גָּדֽוֹל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let me alone, that my wrath may be kindled against them, and that I may destroy them, and I will make of thee a great nation.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sentähden salli nyt minun vihani julmistua heidän päällensä, että minä hukutan heidät: niin minä teen sinun suureksi kansaksi.
verses.king_james: Now therefore let me alone, that my wrath may wax hot against them, and that I may consume them: and I will make of thee a great nation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Moyses autem orabat Dominum Deum suum, dicens : Cur, Domine, irascitur furor tuus contra populum tuum, quem eduxisti de terra Ægypti, in fortitudine magna, et in manu robusta ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְחַ֣ל מֹשֶׁ֔ה אֶת־פְּנֵ֖י יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהָ֑יו וַיֹּ֗אמֶר לָמָ֤ה יְהֹוָה֙ יֶחֱרֶ֤ה אַפְּךָ֙ בְּעַמֶּ֔ךָ אֲשֶׁ֤ר הוֹצֵ֙אתָ֙ מֵאֶ֣רֶץ מִצְרַ֔יִם בְּכֹ֥חַ גָּד֖וֹל וּבְיָ֥ד חֲזָקָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Moses besought the Lord his God, saying: Why, O Lord, is thy indignation enkindled against thy people, whom thou hast brought out of the land of Egypt, with great power, and with a mighty hand?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Moses rukoili hartaasti Herran Jumalansa edessä, ja sanoi: Herra, miksi sinun vihas julmistuu sinun kansan päälle, jonka Egyptin maalta johdatit ulos, suurella voimalla ja väkevällä kädellä?
verses.king_james: And Moses besought the Lord his God, and said, Lord, why doth thy wrath wax hot against thy people, which thou hast brought forth out of the land of Egypt with great power, and with a mighty hand?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne quæso dicant Ægyptii : Callide eduxit eos, ut interficeret in montibus, et deleret e terra : quiescat ira tua, et esto placabilis super nequitia populi tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָ֩מָּה֩ יֹאמְר֨וּ מִצְרַ֜יִם לֵאמֹ֗ר בְּרָעָ֤ה הֽוֹצִיאָם֙ לַהֲרֹ֤ג אֹתָם֙ בֶּֽהָרִ֔ים וּ֨לְכַלֹּתָ֔ם מֵעַ֖ל פְּנֵ֣י הָֽאֲדָמָ֑ה שׁ֚וּב מֵחֲר֣וֹן אַפֶּ֔ךָ וְהִנָּחֵ֥ם עַל־הָרָעָ֖ה לְעַמֶּֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let not the Egyptians say, I beseech thee: He craftily brought them out, that he might kill them in the mountains, and destroy them from the earth: let thy anger cease, and be appeased upon the wickedness of thy people.
verses.finnish_biblia: Miksi Egyptiläiset pitäis puhuman ja sanoman: heidän vahingoksensa hän heitä johdatti ulos, tappaaksensa heitä vuorella, ja hävittääksensä heitä maan päältä? Käänny pois sinun vihas hirmuisuudesta, ja kadu sitä pahaa sinun kansaas vastaan.
verses.king_james: Wherefore should the Egyptians speak, and say, For mischief did he bring them out, to slay them in the mountains, and to consume them from the face of the earth? Turn from thy fierce wrath, and repent of this evil against thy people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Recordare Abraham, Isaac, et Israël servorum tuorum, quibus jurasti per temetipsum, dicens : Multiplicabo semen vestrum sicut stellas cæli ; et universam terram hanc, de qua locutus sum, dabo semini vestro, et possidebitis eam semper.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זְכֹ֡ר לְאַבְרָהָם֩ לְיִצְחָ֨ק וּלְיִשְׂרָאֵ֜ל עֲבָדֶ֗יךָ אֲשֶׁ֨ר נִשְׁבַּ֣עְתָּ לָהֶם֮ בָּךְ֒ וַתְּדַבֵּ֣ר אֲלֵהֶ֔ם אַרְבֶּה֙ אֶֽת־זַרְעֲכֶ֔ם כְּכוֹכְבֵ֖י הַשָּׁמָ֑יִם וְכׇל־הָאָ֨רֶץ הַזֹּ֜את אֲשֶׁ֣ר אָמַ֗רְתִּי אֶתֵּן֙ לְזַרְעֲכֶ֔ם וְנָחֲל֖וּ לְעֹלָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, thy servants, to whom thou sworest by thy own self, saying: I will multiply your seed as the stars of heaven: and this whole land that I have spoken of, I will give to your seed, and you shall possess it for ever:
verses.finnish_biblia: Muista sinun palvelioitas Abrahamia, Isaakia ja Israelia, joille sinä itse kauttas vannoit, ja sanoit heille: minä enennän teidän siemenenne niinkuin taivaan tähdet: ja kaiken tämän maan, josta minä sanoin, annan teidän siemenellenne, ja heidän pitää sen perimän ijankaikkisesti.
verses.king_james: Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, thy servants, to whom thou swarest by thine own self, and saidst unto them, I will multiply your seed as the stars of heaven, and all this land that I have spoken of will I give unto your seed, and they shall inherit it for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Placatusque est Dominus ne faceret malum quod locutus fuerat adversus populum suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּנָּ֖חֶם יְהֹוָ֑ה עַל־הָ֣רָעָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר דִּבֶּ֖ר לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת לְעַמּֽוֹ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord was appeased from doing the evil which he had spoken against his people.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Herra katui sitä pahaa, jonka hän uhkasi tehdä kansallensa.
verses.king_james: And the Lord repented of the evil which he thought to do unto his people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et reversus est Moyses de monte, portans duas tabulas testimonii in manu sua, scriptas ex utraque parte,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּ֜פֶן וַיֵּ֤רֶד מֹשֶׁה֙ מִן־הָהָ֔ר וּשְׁנֵ֛י לֻחֹ֥ת הָעֵדֻ֖ת בְּיָד֑וֹ לֻחֹ֗ת כְּתֻבִים֙ מִשְּׁנֵ֣י עֶבְרֵיהֶ֔ם מִזֶּ֥ה וּמִזֶּ֖ה הֵ֥ם כְּתֻבִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Moses returned from the mount, carrying the two tables of the testimony in his hand, written on both sides,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Moses käänsi itsensä, ja astui alas vuorelta, ja hänen kädessänsä oli kaksi todistuksen taulua: ja taulut olivat kirjoitetut molemmilta puolilta.
verses.king_james: And Moses turned, and went down from the mount, and the two tables of the testimony were in his hand: the tables were written on both their sides; on the one side and on the other were they written.

verses.latin_vulgate: et factas opere Dei : scriptura quoque Dei erat sculpta in tabulis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהַ֨לֻּחֹ֔ת מַעֲשֵׂ֥ה אֱלֹהִ֖ים הֵ֑מָּה וְהַמִּכְתָּ֗ב מִכְתַּ֤ב אֱלֹהִים֙ ה֔וּא חָר֖וּת עַל־הַלֻּחֹֽת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And made by the work of God; the writing also of God was graven in the tables.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja taulut olivat Jumalan teko: ja kirjoitus oli myös Jumalan kirjoitus, kaivettu tauluihin.
verses.king_james: And the tables were the work of God, and the writing was the writing of God, graven upon the tables.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audiens autem Josue tumultum populi vociferantis, dixit ad Moysen : Ululatus pugnæ auditur in castris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁמַ֧ע יְהוֹשֻׁ֛עַ אֶת־ק֥וֹל הָעָ֖ם בְּרֵעֹ֑ה וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אֶל־מֹשֶׁ֔ה ק֥וֹל מִלְחָמָ֖ה בַּֽמַּחֲנֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Josue hearing the noise of the people shouting, said to Moses: The noise of battle is heard in the camp.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska Josua kuuli kansan huudon, sanoi hän Mosekselle: sodan ääni leirissä.
verses.king_james: And when Joshua heard the noise of the people as they shouted, he said unto Moses, There is a noise of war in the camp.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui respondit : Non est clamor adhortantium ad pugnam, neque vociferatio compellentium ad fugam : sed vocem cantantium ego audio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֗אמֶר אֵ֥ין קוֹל֙ עֲנ֣וֹת גְּבוּרָ֔ה וְאֵ֥ין ק֖וֹל עֲנ֣וֹת חֲלוּשָׁ֑ה ק֣וֹל עַנּ֔וֹת אָנֹכִ֖י שֹׁמֵֽעַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But he answered: It is not the cry of men encouraging to fight, nor the shout of men compelling to flee: but I hear the voice of singers.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän vastasi: ei se ole voittajain, eikä voitettuiden huuto: minä kuulen veisaajain äänen.
verses.king_james: And he said, It is not the voice of them that shout for mastery, neither is it the voice of them that cry for being overcome: but the noise of them that sing do I hear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque appropinquasset ad castra, vidit vitulum, et choros : iratusque valde, projecit de manu tabulas, et confregit eas ad radicem montis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְהִ֗י כַּאֲשֶׁ֤ר קָרַב֙ אֶל־הַֽמַּחֲנֶ֔ה וַיַּ֥רְא אֶת־הָעֵ֖גֶל וּמְחֹלֹ֑ת וַיִּֽחַר־אַ֣ף מֹשֶׁ֗ה וַיַּשְׁלֵ֤ךְ מִיָּדָו֙ אֶת־הַלֻּחֹ֔ת וַיְשַׁבֵּ֥ר אֹתָ֖ם תַּ֥חַת הָהָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when he came nigh to the camp, he saw the calf, and the dances: and being very angry, he threw the tables out of his hand, and broke them at the foot of the mount:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja koska hän lähestyi leiriä, näki hän vasikan ja hypyn. Ja Moseksen viha julmistui, ja heitti pois käsistänsä taulut, ja löi ne rikki vuoren alla.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, as soon as he came nigh unto the camp, that he saw the calf, and the dancing: and Moses’ anger waxed hot, and he cast the tables out of his hands, and brake them beneath the mount.

verses.latin_vulgate: arripiensque vitulum quem fecerant, combussit, et contrivit usque ad pulverem, quem sparsit in aquam, et dedit ex eo potum filiis Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקַּ֞ח אֶת־הָעֵ֨גֶל אֲשֶׁ֤ר עָשׂוּ֙ וַיִּשְׂרֹ֣ף בָּאֵ֔שׁ וַיִּטְחַ֖ן עַ֣ד אֲשֶׁר־דָּ֑ק וַיִּ֙זֶר֙ עַל־פְּנֵ֣י הַמַּ֔יִם וַיַּ֖שְׁקְ אֶת־בְּנֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And laying hold of the calf which they had made, he burnt it, and beat it to powder, which he strewed into water, and gave thereof to the children of Israel to drink.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän otti myös vasikan, jonka he tehneet olivat, ja poltti tulella, ja musersi sen tuhaksi; sitte hajoitti hän sen veteen, ja antoi sen Israelin lasten juoda.
verses.king_james: And he took the calf which they had made, and burnt it in the fire, and ground it to powder, and strawed it upon the water, and made the children of Israel drink of it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ad Aaron : Quid tibi fecit hic populus, ut induceres super eum peccatum maximum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר מֹשֶׁה֙ אֶֽל־אַהֲרֹ֔ן מֶֽה־עָשָׂ֥ה לְךָ֖ הָעָ֣ם הַזֶּ֑ה כִּֽי־הֵבֵ֥אתָ עָלָ֖יו חֲטָאָ֥ה גְדֹלָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to Aaron: What has this people done to thee, that thou shouldst bring upon them a most heinous sin?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Moses sanoi Aaronille: mitä tämä kansa on sinulle tehnyt, ettäs niin suuren rikoksen saatit heidän päällensä?
verses.king_james: And Moses said unto Aaron, What did this people unto thee, that thou hast brought so great a sin upon them?

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui ille respondit : Ne indignetur dominus meus : tu enim nosti populum istum, quod pronus sit ad malum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אַהֲרֹ֔ן אַל־יִ֥חַר אַ֖ף אֲדֹנִ֑י אַתָּה֙ יָדַ֣עְתָּ אֶת־הָעָ֔ם כִּ֥י בְרָ֖ע הֽוּא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he answered him: Let not my lord be offended; for thou knowest this people, that they are prone to evil.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Aaron sanoi: älköön minun herrani viha julmistuko: sinä tiedät, että tämä kansa on pahuudessa.
verses.king_james: And Aaron said, Let not the anger of my lord wax hot: thou knowest the people, that they are set on mischief.

verses.latin_vulgate: dixerunt mihi : Fac nobis deos, qui nos præcedant : huic enim Moysi, qui nos eduxit de terra Ægypti, nescimus quid acciderit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמְרוּ לִ֔י עֲשֵׂה־לָ֣נוּ אֱלֹהִ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר יֵלְכ֖וּ לְפָנֵ֑ינוּ כִּי־זֶ֣ה ׀ מֹשֶׁ֣ה הָאִ֗ישׁ אֲשֶׁ֤ר הֶֽעֱלָ֙נוּ֙ מֵאֶ֣רֶץ מִצְרַ֔יִם לֹ֥א יָדַ֖עְנוּ מֶה־הָ֥יָה לֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: They said to me: make us gods, that may go before us; for as to this Moses, who brought us forth out of the land of Egypt, we know not what is befallen him.
verses.finnish_biblia: He sanoivat minulle: tee meille jumalia, jotka meidän edellemme kävisivät; sillä emme tiedä, mitä tälle miehelle Mosekselle tapahtunut on, joka meidän Egyptin maalta johdatti.
verses.king_james: For they said unto me, Make us gods, which shall go before us: for as for this Moses, the man that brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quibus ego dixi : Quis vestrum habet aurum ? Tulerunt, et dederunt mihi : et projeci illud in ignem, egressusque est hic vitulus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֹמַ֤ר לָהֶם֙ לְמִ֣י זָהָ֔ב הִתְפָּרָ֖קוּ וַיִּתְּנוּ־לִ֑י וָאַשְׁלִכֵ֣הוּ בָאֵ֔שׁ וַיֵּצֵ֖א הָעֵ֥גֶל הַזֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I said to them: Which of you hath any gold? and they took and brought it to me; and I cast it into the fire, and this calf came out.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joille minä sanoin: jolla on kultaa, reväiskään sen pois; ja he antoivat sen minulle, ja minä heitin sen tuleen, ja siitä tuli tämä vasikka.
verses.king_james: And I said unto them, Whosoever hath any gold, let them break it off. So they gave it me: then I cast it into the fire, and there came out this calf.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videns ergo Moyses populum quod esset nudatus (spoliaverat enim eum Aaron propter ignominiam sordis, et inter hostes nudum constituerat),
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֤רְא מֹשֶׁה֙ אֶת־הָעָ֔ם כִּ֥י פָרֻ֖עַ ה֑וּא כִּֽי־פְרָעֹ֣ה אַהֲרֹ֔ן לְשִׁמְצָ֖ה בְּקָמֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when Moses saw that the people were naked, (for Aaron had stripped them by occasion of the shame of the filth, and had set them naked among their enemies)
verses.finnish_biblia: Kun Moses näki, että kansa oli paljastettu; sillä Aaron oli heidän paljastanut häväistykseksi heidän vihollisillensa,
verses.king_james: And when Moses saw that the people were naked; (for Aaron had made them naked unto their shame among their enemies:)

verses.latin_vulgate: et stans in porta castrorum, ait : Si quis est Domini, jungatur mihi. Congregatique sunt ad eum omnes filii Levi :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּעֲמֹ֤ד מֹשֶׁה֙ בְּשַׁ֣עַר הַֽמַּחֲנֶ֔ה וַיֹּ֕אמֶר מִ֥י לַיהֹוָ֖ה אֵלָ֑י וַיֵּאָסְפ֥וּ אֵלָ֖יו כׇּל־בְּנֵ֥י לֵוִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then standing in the gate of the camp, he said: If any man be on the Lord's side, let him join with me. And all the sons of Levi gathered themselves together unto him:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Moses seisoi leirin portissa ja sanoi: joka on Herran oma, se tulkaan minun tyköni; niin kokoontuivat hänen tykönsä kaikki Levin pojat,
verses.king_james: Then Moses stood in the gate of the camp, and said, Who is on the Lord’s side? let him come unto me. And all the sons of Levi gathered themselves together unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: quibus ait : Hæc dicit Dominus Deus Israël : Ponat vir gladium super femur suum : ite, et redite de porta usque ad portam per medium castrorum, et occidat unusquisque fratrem, et amicum, et proximum suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר לָהֶ֗ם כֹּֽה־אָמַ֤ר יְהֹוָה֙ אֱלֹהֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל שִׂ֥ימוּ אִישׁ־חַרְבּ֖וֹ עַל־יְרֵכ֑וֹ עִבְר֨וּ וָשׁ֜וּבוּ מִשַּׁ֤עַר לָשַׁ֙עַר֙ בַּֽמַּחֲנֶ֔ה וְהִרְג֧וּ אִֽישׁ־אֶת־אָחִ֛יו וְאִ֥ישׁ אֶת־רֵעֵ֖הוּ וְאִ֥ישׁ אֶת־קְרֹבֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to them: Thus saith the Lord God of Israel: Put every man his sword upon his thigh: go, and return from gate to gate through the midst of the camp, and let every man kill his brother, and friend, and neighbour.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joille hän sanoi: näin sanoo Herra Israelin Jumala: jokainen sitokaan miekan kupeillensa: vaeltakaat lävitse, ja palaitkaat portista niin porttiin leirissä, ja tappakaat itsekukin veljensä, ja kukin ystävänsä, ja kukin lähimmäisensä.
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, Thus saith the Lord God of Israel, Put every man his sword by his side, and go in and out from gate to gate throughout the camp, and slay every man his brother, and every man his companion, and every man his neighbour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Feceruntque filii Levi juxta sermonem Moysi, cecideruntque in die illa quasi viginti tria millia hominum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּֽעֲשׂ֥וּ בְנֵֽי־לֵוִ֖י כִּדְבַ֣ר מֹשֶׁ֑ה וַיִּפֹּ֤ל מִן־הָעָם֙ בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֔וּא כִּשְׁלֹ֥שֶׁת אַלְפֵ֖י אִֽישׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the sons of Levi did according to the words of Moses, and there were slain that day about three and twenty thousand men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Levin pojat tekivät Moseksen käskyn jälkeen: ja sinä päivänä lankesi kansasta liki kolmetuhatta miestä.
verses.king_james: And the children of Levi did according to the word of Moses: and there fell of the people that day about three thousand men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Moyses : Consecrastis manus vestras hodie Domino, unusquisque in filio, et in fratre suo, ut detur vobis benedictio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר מֹשֶׁ֗ה מִלְא֨וּ יֶדְכֶ֤ם הַיּוֹם֙ לַֽיהֹוָ֔ה כִּ֛י אִ֥ישׁ בִּבְנ֖וֹ וּבְאָחִ֑יו וְלָתֵ֧ת עֲלֵיכֶ֛ם הַיּ֖וֹם בְּרָכָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Moses said: You have consecrated your hands this day to the Lord, every man in his son and in his brother, that a blessing may be given to you.
verses.finnish_biblia: Moses sanoi: pyhittäkäät tänäpänä teidän kätenne Herralle, itsekukin pojassansa ja veljessänsä, että hän tänäpänä antais teille siunauksen.
verses.king_james: For Moses had said, Consecrate yourselves to day to the Lord, even every man upon his son, and upon his brother; that he may bestow upon you a blessing this day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Facto autem altero die, locutus est Moyses ad populum : Peccastis peccatum maximum : ascendam ad Dominum, si quomodo quivero eum deprecari pro scelere vestro.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְהִי֙ מִֽמׇּחֳרָ֔ת וַיֹּ֤אמֶר מֹשֶׁה֙ אֶל־הָעָ֔ם אַתֶּ֥ם חֲטָאתֶ֖ם חֲטָאָ֣ה גְדֹלָ֑ה וְעַתָּה֙ אֶֽעֱלֶ֣ה אֶל־יְהֹוָ֔ה אוּלַ֥י אֲכַפְּרָ֖ה בְּעַ֥ד חַטַּאתְכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when the next day was come, Moses spoke to the people: You have sinned a very great sin: I will go up to the Lord, if by any means I may be able to entreat him for your crime.
verses.finnish_biblia: Toisna päivänä sanoi Moses kansalle: te olette tehneet suuren synnin; ja nyt minä astun ylös Herran tykö, jos minä mitämaks taidan sovittaa teidän rikoksenne.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass on the morrow, that Moses said unto the people, Ye have sinned a great sin: and now I will go up unto the Lord; peradventure I shall make an atonement for your sin.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reversusque ad Dominum, ait : Obsecro, peccavit populus iste peccatum maximum, feceruntque sibi deos aureos : aut dimitte eis hanc noxam,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֧שׇׁב מֹשֶׁ֛ה אֶל־יְהֹוָ֖ה וַיֹּאמַ֑ר אָ֣נָּ֗א חָטָ֞א הָעָ֤ם הַזֶּה֙ חֲטָאָ֣ה גְדֹלָ֔ה וַיַּֽעֲשׂ֥וּ לָהֶ֖ם אֱלֹהֵ֥י זָהָֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And returning to the Lord, he said: I beseech thee: this people hath sinned a heinous sin, and they have made to themselves gods of gold: either forgive them this trespass,
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Moses palasi Herran tykö, ja sanoi: minä rukoilen; tämä kansa teki suuren synnin, ja he tekivät itsellensä kultaisia jumalia.
verses.king_james: And Moses returned unto the Lord, and said, Oh, this people have sinned a great sin, and have made them gods of gold.

verses.latin_vulgate: aut si non facis, dele me de libro tuo quem scripsisti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֖ה אִם־תִּשָּׂ֣א חַטָּאתָ֑ם וְאִם־אַ֕יִן מְחֵ֣נִי נָ֔א מִֽסִּפְרְךָ֖ אֲשֶׁ֥ר כָּתָֽבְתָּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Or if thou do not, strike me out of the book that thou hast written.
verses.finnish_biblia: Nyt siis anna heidän rikoksensa anteeksi; mutta jollei, niin pyyhi minut nyt pois sinun kirjastas, jonkas kirjoittanut olet.
verses.king_james: Yet now, if thou wilt forgive their sin—; and if not, blot me, I pray thee, out of thy book which thou hast written.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui respondit Dominus : Qui peccaverit mihi, delebo eum de libro meo :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר יְהֹוָ֖ה אֶל־מֹשֶׁ֑ה מִ֚י אֲשֶׁ֣ר חָֽטָא־לִ֔י אֶמְחֶ֖נּוּ מִסִּפְרִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord answered him: He that hath sinned against me, him will I strike out of my book:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra sanoi Mosekselle: joka minua vastaan syntiä tekee, sen minä pyyhin minun kirjastani.
verses.king_james: And the Lord said unto Moses, Whosoever hath sinned against me, him will I blot out of my book.

verses.latin_vulgate: tu autem vade, et duc populum istum quo locutus sum tibi : angelus meus præcedet te. Ego autem in die ultionis visitabo et hoc peccatum eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֞ה לֵ֣ךְ ׀ נְחֵ֣ה אֶת־הָעָ֗ם אֶ֤ל אֲשֶׁר־דִּבַּ֙רְתִּי֙ לָ֔ךְ הִנֵּ֥ה מַלְאָכִ֖י יֵלֵ֣ךְ לְפָנֶ֑יךָ וּבְי֣וֹם פׇּקְדִ֔י וּפָקַדְתִּ֥י עֲלֵהֶ֖ם חַטָּאתָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: But go thou, and lead this people whither I have told thee: my angel shall go before thee. And I in the day of revenge will visit this sin also of theirs.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin mene sinä nyt, ja johdata kansa sille sialle, josta minä olen sinulle puhunut: Katsos, minun enkelini käy sinun edelläs. Mutta minun etsikkopäivänäni kostan minä heidän rikoksensa.
verses.king_james: Therefore now go, lead the people unto the place of which I have spoken unto thee: behold, mine Angel shall go before thee: nevertheless in the day when I visit I will visit their sin upon them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Percussit ergo Dominus populum pro reatu vituli, quem fecerat Aaron.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּגֹּ֥ף יְהֹוָ֖ה אֶת־הָעָ֑ם עַ֚ל אֲשֶׁ֣ר עָשׂ֣וּ אֶת־הָעֵ֔גֶל אֲשֶׁ֥ר עָשָׂ֖ה אַהֲרֹֽן׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: The Lord therefore struck the people for the guilt, on occasion of the calf which Aaron had made.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja niin löi Herra kansaa; että he olivat tehneet vasikan, jonka Aaron teki.
verses.king_james: And the Lord plagued the people, because they made the calf, which Aaron made.
